item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everybody knows that SCP-3908 is SCP-3908.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not such a bad guy.

this feels a bit subjective for official foundation documentation. i'd cut it

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
Oh, and a further correction

that is not a picture by Dr. Zalama

it is a picture of Dr. Zalama

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i feel like reading this is making one side of my brain think one thing and the other side think another thing and neither of them make any sense

item.title: Appreciation Note (Also Stop Thinking About This.)
item.content:encoded:
First and foremost, I want to say that I very much appreciate all the critical acclaim this SCP has received, and I cannot thank you all enough for escalating this piece to 200+ upvotes.

And now for my second and main point: Everyone talks about this SCP as if it had some deeper meaning. I am here to state that it really did not. I would estimate I dedicated 8-16 hours of brain energy to this SCP. I probably spent about 2 weeks, at most, writing this. If you have thought about this SCP, chances are you have thought about it more than I have. My humble request is less credit for this.

Now that is done and dusted, I will return to writing smut and D&D 5e homebrew races.

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MAZalama.jpg

(correction)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something extra horrifying about not only losing your identity, but having that loss extend to your emotions and your actions as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creative piece. Especially that last part, it feels oddly wholesome to me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the second interview, the skip refers to himself twice.

"SCP-3908: Alright, alright, that I think I can do."

Since every time he refers to himself he presents to anomalous effect, wouldn't this happen here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt really bad for SCP-3908, and felt like if I were in that situation, I would be terrified and always worrisome about what I say.

Honestly deserves to be looked at more, since he's just an ordinary guy who got antimeme'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kilogram only has one L

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man I have never sympathized with a skip in such a short period of time. And that photo… how on Earth did you manage to find a picture of the nicest looking guy on the planet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3908 is SCP-3908 being SCP-3908, while it might not quite be SCP-3908, it is most certainly SCP-3908, alongside SCP-3908.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the entry dealt with the still-important immediate problem of being able to just write his containment procedures and description. Also, it would be tough to talk about what you're describing without getting into the SCP-3908 is SCP-3908 problem, and would have harmed the charming brevity of this entry.

Might make a good tale, tho. (Not suggesting anyone write it, just musing aloud.)

-Kirt

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great Work, I liked how SCP-3908 was SCP-3908

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good job intersplicing clinical tone with SCP-3908 being SCP-3908. However, the fact that there was an SCP out in the world that had to be referred to by its designation has implications that are simply not addressed: namely, a broken masquerade situation. He was around long enough to gain a nickname, so surely someone would start asking questions. Also, it seems the Foundation just gave up on finding out Skip's origin, which, you know, would be invaluable info.

Maybe they would at least set up a web crawler to try and suppress public knowledge on SCP-3908?

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
The picture of the human was taken by Miguel Ángel Zalama.

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/9/92/MAZalama.jpg

Who, in contrast to this article, is not SCP-3908.

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
the picture of SCP-3908 is obviously SCP-3908.

but please source the picture of the human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Missed this at the time but glad I caught it now. This is exactly my sort of thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What can be easily read between the lines makes this great.
While the personal nature of the last addendum is a bit atypical, I feel like the foundation at that point just took whatever document does not contain the anomaly.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
woah this is

cool

I'm always a sucker for meta articles, especially if done right. This is an amazing concept with the writing style, and I like the note at the end that doesn't display anomalous properties.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote is upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate the subtlety here. The SCP's frustration is well depicted, and Witts is more humane than Foundation personnel often are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the almost anticlimatic dip that he has anger issues and that it's a normal human flaw that creates relatively normal human problems. There's a certain poetic rhythm here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that I think I can do.

Also, this one seemed like it would be disallowed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuckin' brilliant.

[SCP-3908 is SCP-3908. The table is on the other side of the room now.]

Goddamn.

I didn't think meta or antimemes could be done simply, but you have done just that!

(Though should the line "The reason why I was friendless" be there? It seems like the kind of thing that he wouldn't be able to say.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last section is the description re-written into a form that gets around the anomaly- You can't directly refer to 3908 with it turning into a bunch of inane 'SCP-3908 is SCP-3908' statements, but they figured out he could be indirectly referred to- hence why the last bit is a bunch of indirect and grammatically unrelated statements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whatever this is trying to do is not clicking for me. I am really split on meta skips; they either knock it out of the park or flop. This feels like one of those metas that just fail and try too hard. The main thing I don't like about this is the fact that SCP-3908 knows it's an SCP and keeps going on about it.

Also what was the point of the last section with the old man? It feels out of place

-1

item.title: I have changed my mind.
item.content:encoded:
I have reread this a few times now and it has grown on me. Your dialogues were excellent.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You managed to pull this off. Really nice job. Being unable to talk about what 3908 is doing, then implying through the consequences, was a beautiful touch. +1

item.title: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who dealt with me asking for critiques on this thing.

Remember, that SCP-3908 isn't a bad guy. And neither are you.
